Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Mci Metro Access Transmission Service Corp.
Off Calendar Dec-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances In Open court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MCI METRO ACCESS TRANSMISSION SERVICE CORP's Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Mci Metro Access Transmission Service Corp.

Defendant MCI Metro Access Transportation Service Corp's unopposed Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant MCI Metro Access Transmission Service Corp is GRANTED.

Defendant moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), which allows a court to set aside a default or a judgment based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect.

The court finds good cause and orders the default against it set aside and grants Moving Defendant 10 court days leave to file an answer substantially identical to the answer submitted with its moving papers, the time starting upon entry of this order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-21-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant MCI Metro Access Transmission Service Corp.

Dept.505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:46:23 a.m., pausing at 10:07:41 a.m. resuming at 10:08:04 a.m. and concluding at 10:14:29 a.m. (D505).

Kelechi C. Emeziem, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Debbie Atmaja.
Plaintiff Debbie Atmaja was present, placed under oath and testified.
Defendant MCI Metro Access Transmission Service Corp. not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for Personal Injury filed Jun-10-2020.
Proof of personal service served as to Defendant on Nov-29-2022.
Default entered as to Defendant MCI Metro Access Transmission Service Corp. on Mar-21-2023.
Defendants JPI Construction, Mas Tec Inc and Mas Tec North America Inc and does 1-100 dismissed on Oct-06-2023.

Counsel to provide a summary, offer of proof of the prayer in the complaint.
Court examines Plaintiff.
The Court is willing to grant the judgment but modifies the amount sought according to proof presented to the court.
The request for interest is denied.

Good cause appearing, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has proven its prima facie case and rules in favor of the Plaintiff against the Defendant as follows:

The Plaintiff shall recover from the Defendant:
Medical Bills $590.00
Lost Wages $1,200.00
Pain and Suffering $20,000.00
Costs $0
Attorney's Fees $0
Interest $0
Total Judgment $21,790.00

Proposed judgment as modified signed and executed this day.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Continued To May-23-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint, Ccp Section 583.420(A)(1)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
KELECHI CHARLES EMEZIEM, Esq., EMEZIEM & 0THERS, 510-628-0400 Keemeziem@emeziemlaw.com, for Plaintiff DEBBIE ATMAJA
KEVIN P. CODY , Esq., ROPERS MAJESKI PC, 408.287.6262 kevin.cody@ropers.com for Defendants JPI CONSTRUCTION, INC., MAS TEC, INC., and MAS TEC NORTH AMERICA, INC.
No other appearances.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT JPI CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint, Ccp Section 583.420(A)(1)

JPI Construction, Inc.'s "motion to dismiss for failure to serve summons and complaint, Code of Civil Procedure section 583.420(a)(1)" is denied.

CCP 583.420(a)(1) gives a court discretion to "dismiss an action" if "[s]ervice is not made within two years after the action is commenced against the defendant." Both sides recognize that, for a Doe defendant (as here), the clock starts with the original complaint's filing and that plaintiff may show "excusable delay."

Here, the slip-and-fall plaintiff was pro per when she sued in December 2019. (Emeziem Dec. 1:25-26.) Covid-19 then shut down court houses and ground legal machinery to a halt. (Id. at 2:2-3.) When plaintiff did retain a lawyer, he performed discovery of the named defendants, during which he learned JPI could be a named as a Doe. (Id. at 2:6-15.) However, plaintiff's counsel then had difficulty for many months getting the Doe amendment filed at the courthouse. (Id. at 2:16-24.) When the amendment finally was filed in November 2022, JPI was quickly served. (Id. at 2:24-27.)

Counsel did not always perform with blazing speed, but disputes are best resolved on their merits and - in a discretionary setting - a lawyer's sins need not be visited on his client.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)

Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint, Ccp Section 583.420(A)(1)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
KELECHI CHARLES EMEZIEM, Esq., EMEZIEM & 0THERS, 510-628-0400 Keemeziem@emeziemlaw.com, for Plaintiff DEBBIE ATMAJA

No other appearances.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - DEFENDANTS MAS TEC, INC., MAS TEC NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Summons And Complaint, Ccp Section 583.420(A)(1)

Mas Tec, Inc. and Mas Tec North America, Inc.'s "motion to dismiss for failure to serve summons and complaint, Code of Civil Procedure section 583.420(a)(1)" is denied.

CCP 583.420(a)(1) gives a court discretion to "dismiss an action" if "[s]ervice is not made within two years after the action is commenced against the defendant." Both sides recognize that, for a Doe defendant (as here), the clock starts with the original complaint's filing and that plaintiff may show "excusable delay."

Here, the slip-and-fall plaintiff was pro per when she sued in December 2019. (Emeziem Dec. 1:25-26.) Covid-19 then shut down court houses and ground legal machinery to a halt. (Id. at 2:2-3.) When plaintiff did retain a lawyer, he performed discovery of the named defendants, during which he learned the Mas Tec entities could be named as a Does. (Id. at 2:6-15.)

However, plaintiff's counsel then had difficulty for many months getting the Doe amendments filed at the courthouse. (Id. at 2:16-24.) When the amendment finally was filed in November 2022, the Mas Tec entities were quickly served. (Id. at 2:24-27.)

Counsel did not always perform with blazing speed, but disputes are best resolved on their merits and - in a discretionary setting - a lawyer's sins need not be visited on his client.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
